notes: Win32 surface coverage is heuristic source inspection for named API families.
cargo_audit_clean is direct RustSec candidate advisory status, not a full cargo-audit transitive graph run.

license_findings: crates.io API returned 404; candidate is only available as an OpenAI Codex workspace crate.
candidates.open_issues_basis: GitHub repository open_issues_count; workspace-crate-specific issue counts are not available without a separate search heuristic.
candidates.maintainer_count_basis: GitHub contributors count from the first page because workspace crate owners are not published on crates.io.
candidates.cargo_audit_basis: cargo-audit CLI was not required; direct crate advisories were checked in RustSec advisory-db. Transitive dependency advisories are not fully enumerated for this workspace crate.
candidates.dependency_count_basis: Declared direct dependency names parsed from the workspace crate Cargo.toml; not a full resolved cargo tree.

license_findings: GitHub repository license API reports NOASSERTION even though Cargo.toml and LICENSE identify MIT.
Repository default branch Cargo.toml version 0.13.10 differs from latest crates.io version 0.13.3; MT-046 should consume only the audited registry version unless a new audit is run.
Some historical rappct versions are yanked on crates.io; current audited version is not yanked.
candidates.open_issues_basis: GitHub repository open_issues_count, which may include pull requests.
candidates.cargo_audit_basis: Direct crate advisories checked in RustSec advisory-db; cargo-audit CLI was not required for this governance proof command.
candidates.dependency_count_basis: crates.io version dependency API count; not a fully resolved cargo tree.

decision.rationale: MT-046 remediation selected rappct 0.13.3 after supplemental source-level validation. It is a crates.io-published MIT crate, compiles on the local Windows toolchain, and provides AppContainer profile creation, LPAC support, AppContainer ACL grants, stdio-pipe launch, and Job Object kill-on-close support. rappct does not expose a public Restricted Token API, so MT-046 uses rappct as the audited substrate and closes the missing trinity layer with a narrow local Win32 bridge confined to restricted_appcontainer.rs and job_object_wrap.rs. The alternate codex-windows-sandbox candidate is not published on crates.io, is a Codex workspace-internal crate with path dependencies/helper-binary assumptions, and does not provide AppContainer/LPAC coverage as a drop-in backend.
decision.license_acceptance_path: No GPL-3.0 or proprietary license blocker was found. rappct 0.13.3 is MIT-licensed and acceptable for product dependency adoption under the MT-046 residual-risk record. codex-windows-sandbox remains Apache-2.0 but is not selected because it is not a drop-in published dependency for this product path.

sources_checked: local Cargo registry source for rappct 0.13.3
local CreateRestrictedToken SID probe for AppContainer/capability SID validity
proof_commands: cargo check --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration

cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration --test windows_native_jail_adapter_tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture

cargo test --manifest-path src/backend/handshake_core/Cargo.toml -p handshake_core --target-dir ../Handshake_Artifacts/handshake-cargo-target --target x86_64-pc-windows-msvc --features win-native-integration --test sandbox_selection_tests --test sandbox_promotion_binding_tests -- --test-threads=1 --nocapture
static test in windows_native_jail_adapter_tests validates CreateRestrictedToken/CreateProcessAsUserW/SECURITY_CAPABILITIES/JOB_LIST calls are present and confined to the audited wrapper files
residual_risks: rappct lacks public Restricted Token API coverage; MT-046 closes this at the product layer through a narrow local Win32 bridge instead of accepting a missing runtime boundary.

LPAC attribute application is attempted but runtime token telemetry reports is_lpac=false when composed with CreateProcessAsUserW on the validated host; MT-046 acceptance is AppContainer + Restricted Token + Job Object, so LPAC is retained as telemetry rather than overclaimed.
rappct ACL grant helper has no matching revoke helper; AppContainer profile deletion and temp-path isolation are used for cleanup, but file ACL cleanup remains a hardening watchpoint.
LoopbackOnly and host allowlist networking are rejected loudly until a loopback-exemption or WFP allowlist enforcement path is implemented without broad internetClient capability.
mitigations: Direct windows-sys Win32 isolation calls are confined to restricted_appcontainer.rs and job_object_wrap.rs and covered by a static contract test.
Runtime token probe asserts is_appcontainer=true and is_restricted=true.
Runtime file probe asserts read-only bind write denial, read-write bind success, outside existing read denial, and outside existing write denial.
Runtime job probe asserts TerminateJobObject kills the child tree.
Runtime network probe asserts DenyAll outbound TCP cannot connect.
Adapter initialization probes AppContainer support with unique profile names, retries transient profile API failures, and serializes profile create/delete calls.
Promotion binding requires WindowsNativeJail runtime availability plus VeryStrong filesystem isolation before accepting win32_native_fidelity evidence.
LoopbackOnly and host allowlist network policies fail loudly instead of overclaiming enforcement.
